paper_title: FT-IR and Morphology Analysis of Composite PCM

methods.description: FT-IR analysis is used to determine the chemical composition and interactions within the composite PCM, specifically to identify the presence and interaction of CA-LA eutectic and diatomite filter aid.

steps.description: Analyze the FT-IR spectra of CA-LA eutectic, diatomite filter aid, and CA-LA/diatomite filter aid composite PCM.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze FT-IR spectra to identify chemical composition
inputs: FT-IR spectra of diatomite filter aid

steps.description: Identify characteristic absorption peaks and compare them to known peaks of CA-LA eutectic and diatomite filter aid.
steps.searchable_summary: identify and compare absorption peaks in FT-IR spectra

steps.description: Confirm that no new absorption peaks indicate a physical combination without chemical reaction.
steps.searchable_summary: validate absence of new peaks to confirm physical combination

methods.method_name: Morphology and Structure Analysis of Composite PCM
methods.description: Morphology and structure analysis using SEM and BJH methods to determine the physical structure and pore characteristics of the composite PCM.

steps.description: Examine SEM images of diatomite filter aid and CA-LA/diatomite filter aid form-stable PCM.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze SEM images to assess morphology
inputs: SEM images of diatomite filter aid

steps.description: Perform BJH method analysis to determine micropore diameter and volume.
steps.searchable_summary: use BJH method to analyze micropore characteristics

steps.description: Compare micropore volume and specific surface area before and after filling with CA-LA eutectic.
steps.searchable_summary: compare micropore volume and surface area changes

steps.description: Infer the ability of micropore structure to absorb CA-LA eutectic by capillary force and surface tension.
